{"content":[{"type":"text","name":"EXIT_CODE","text":"0"},{"type":"text","name":"STDOUT","text":"local local_share = require("ask-openai.config.local_share")
local LlamaServerClient = require("ask-openai.backends.llama_cpp.llama_server_client")

local M = {}

-- cache for model names per base url (host:port)
-- stores { value = string|nil, ts = integer }
local _model_cache = {}

--- Query the llama-server for the model name running at the given base URL.
--- Uses the `/v1/models` endpoint and caches the result per URL.
--- @param base_url string The base URL of the llama-server (e.g. "http://paxy.lan:8012")
--- @return string|nil The model abbreviation, or nil if server request failed. Returns "OFFLINE" for unknown models.
function M.get_llama_server_model(base_url)
    -- TODO figure out why ltn12 and socket.http packages together are not working on wesdemos, but working fine on wes user... WTF
    --   my innermost JSON lua module is just returning NOTHING on all requests as wesdemos... FUUUUU
    --   might have smth to do with neovim v0.12 install (then remove) and lua5.1 missing now (kinda)... though luarocks on both users has the packages I need... I have no damn idea anymore

    -- TODO test and verify this does what I want, then use it to show the model in status bar (cache first load)
    --    TODO test below from gptoss
    --    btw the below works for wes user only

    -- TODO cache response for a time period even if nil... would allow checking again without overwhelming server... i.e. if in lualine components
    local cached = _model_cache[base_url]
    if cached then
        local timeout = cached.value == nil and 1 or 10
        if os.time() - cached.ts < timeout then
            return cached.value
        end
    end

    local response = LlamaServerClient.get_models(base_url)
    if not response or response.code ~= 200 then
        return nil
    end

    local body = response.body
    local model_name = nil
    if type(body) == "table" then
        if body.models and #body.models > 0 then
            local first_model = body.models[1]
            if type(first_model) == "table" and first_model.name then
                model_name = first_model.name
            end
        else
            model_name = "no model.id"
        end
    end

    -- Model name abbreviation lookup table
    local model_name_map = {
        ["ggml-org/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF:Q8_0"] = "qwen3.6mtp",
        ["ggml-org/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF:Q8_0"] = "qwen3",
        ["ggml-org/gpt-oss-120b-GGUF"] = "gptoss",
    }

    -- Apply abbreviation from lookup table, or fall back to "OFFLINE" sentinel
    model_name = model_name_map[model_name] or "OFFLINE"

    _model_cache[base_url] = { value = model_name, ts = os.time() }
    return model_name
end



---@class Provider
---@field get_bearer_token fun(): string
---@field check fun() # optional
---@field get_cmdline_base_url fun(): string # optional

--- ask-openai options
---@class AskOpenAIOptions
---@field model string
---@field provider string
---@field copilot CopilotOptions
---@field api_url string|nil
local default_options = {

    keymaps = {
        cmdline_ask = "<C-b>",
    },

    provider = "copilot",
    -- provider = "keyless",
    -- provider = function() ... end,

    ---@class CopilotOptions
    ---@field timeout number
    ---@field proxy string|nil
    ---@field insecure boolean
    copilot = {
        timeout = 30000,
        proxy = nil,
        insecure = false,
    },

    --- must be set to full endpoint URL, e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions
    api_url = nil,
    use_api_groq = false,
    use_api_openai = false,
    use_api_ollama = false, -- prefer this for local models unless not supported
    -- FYI rationale for use_* is to get completion without users needing to require a lookup or list of endpoints to complete

    -- request parameters:
    model = "gpt-4o",
    max_tokens = 2000, -- higher when using thinking models like gptoss120b
    -- PRN temperature
    -- in future, if add other ask helpers then I can move these into a nested table like copilot options

    tmp = {
        commandline = {
            -- TODO migrate here from top level (also consider other scenarios where I might want to configure different backend/mode/etc)
        },
        predictions = {
            keymaps = {
                accept_all = "<Tab>",
                accept_line = "<C-right>",
                accept_word = "<M-right>",
                resume_stream = "<M-up>",
                pause_stream = "<M-down>",
                new_prediction = "<M-Tab>",
            },
        }
    }

}

local cached_options = default_options

---@param user_options AskOpenAIOptions
local function set_user_options(user_options)
    cached_options = vim.tbl_deep_extend("force", default_options, user_options or {})
end

---@return AskOpenAIOptions
function M.get_options()
    return cached_options
end

function M.print_verbose(msg, ...)
    if local_share.is_trace_logging_enabled() then
        print(msg, ...)
    end
end

--- @class Endpoint
--- @field name string
--- @field base_url string

--- @return table<string, Endpoint>
function M.get_endpoints()
    local gptoss_url = "http://ask.lan:8013"
    local qwen3_url = "http://ask.lan:8012"

    local gptoss_model = M.get_llama_server_model(gptoss_url)
    local qwen3_model = M.get_llama_server_model(qwen3_url)

    local endpoints = {
        agents = {
            name = qwen3_model or "OFFLINE",
            base_url = qwen3_url,
        },
        rewrite = {
            name = gptoss_model or "OFFLINE",
            base_url = gptoss_url,
        },
        cmdline = {
            name = nil,
            base_url = M.get_cmdline_base_url(),
        },
        gptoss = {
            name = gptoss_model or "OFFLINE",
            base_url = gptoss_url,
        },
        summarizer = {
            name = gptoss_model or "OFFLINE",
            base_url = gptoss_url,
        },
        qwen3 = {
            name = qwen3_model or "OFFLINE",
            base_url = qwen3_url,
        },
    }
    return endpoints
end

local function _get_provider()
    -- FYI prints below only show on first run b/c provider is cached by get_provider() so NBD to add that extra info which is useful to know config is correct w/o toggling verbose on and getting a wall of logs
    if cached_options.provider == "copilot" then
        print("AskOpenAI: Using Copilot")
        return require("ask-openai.providers.copilot")
    elseif cached_options.provider == "keyless" then
        print("AskOpenAI: Using Keyless")
        return require("ask-openai.providers.keyless")
    elseif type(cached_options.provider) == "function" then
        print("AskOpenAI: Using BYOK function")
        return require("ask-openai.providers.byok")(cached_options.provider)
    else
        error("AskOpenAI: Invalid provider")
    end
end

---@type Provider
local cached_provider = nil

---@return Provider
function M.get_provider()
    if cached_provider == nil then
        cached_provider = _get_provider()
    end
    return cached_provider
end

function M.get_cmdline_base_url()
    local _provider = M.get_provider()
    if _provider.get_cmdline_base_url then
        return _provider.get_cmdline_base_url()
    end

    if cached_options.api_url then
        return cached_options.api_url
    elseif cached_options.use_api_groq then
        return "https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions"
    elseif cached_options.use_api_ollama then
        return "http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions"
    elseif cached_options.use_api_openai then
        return "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions"
    else
        -- default to openai
        return "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions"
    end
end

function M.get_key_from_stdout(cmd_string)
    local handle = io.popen(cmd_string)
    if not handle then
        return nil
    end

    -- remove any extra whitespace
    local api_key = handle:read("*a"):gsub("%s+", "")

    handle:close()

    -- ok if empty/nil, will be checked
    return api_key
end

function M.get_validated_bearer_token()
    local bearer_token = M.get_provider().get_bearer_token()

    -- TODO can I reuse check() for these same checks? or just remove these and rely on checkhealth alone?
    -- VALIDATION => could push into provider, but especially w/ func provider it's good to do generic validation/tracing across all providers
    if bearer_token == nil then
        return 'Ask failed, bearer_token is nil'
    elseif bearer_token == "" then
        -- don't fail, just add to tracing
        M.print_verbose("FYI bearer_token is empty")
    end

    return bearer_token
end

function M.check()
    local _provider = M.get_provider()
    if _provider.check then
        _provider.check()
    end

    local bearer_token = _provider.get_bearer_token()
    if bearer_token == nil then
        vim.health.error("bearer_token is nil")
    elseif bearer_token == "" then
        vim.health.error("bearer_token is empty")
    else
        vim.health.ok("bearer_token retrieved")
        if local_share.is_trace_logging_enabled() then
            -- TODO extract mask function and test it, try to submit to plenary? or does plenary have one?
            local len = string.len(bearer_token)
            local num = math.min(5, math.floor(len * 0.07)) -- first and last 7%, max of 5 chars
            if num < 1 then
                vim.health.info("bearer_token too short to show start/end")
            else
                local masked = string.sub(bearer_token, 1, num)
                    .. "*****"
                    .. string.sub(bearer_token, -num)
                vim.health.info("bearer_token: " .. masked)
            end
        end
    end

    local options = {
        chat_url = M.get_cmdline_base_url(),
        provider_type = M.get_options().provider,
        model = M.get_options().model,
    }
    vim.health.info(vim.inspect(options))
end

function M.setup(user_options)
    set_user_options(user_options)
    local_share.setup()
end

M.local_share = local_share

return M
"}]}

let's set --connect-timeout too actually... I want that low b/c that would be the fastest way to abort if the server is not online which is most likely in my case to be a problem... if it is online then it will almost always respond quickly for model name... so connect timeout should be low for getting name... what value would you suggest for connect timeout?

messages.reasoning_content:
For `--connect-timeout`, the goal is to fail fast when the server is unreachable. A connection attempt either:
- Succeeds quickly (server is up)
- Fails quickly (connection refused, host unreachable)
- Hangs (firewall dropping packets, network unreachable)

For `--connect-timeout`, 3 seconds is reasonable. That gives enough time for a connection to establish on a healthy local network, but fails fast if the host is down or a firewall is silently dropping. Anything lower (1s) might be too aggressive on a busy network; anything higher defeats the purpose.

Since we're already doing 3s for `--max-time`, making `--connect-timeout` the same or slightly lower makes sense. I'd suggest 3s for connect-timeout as well - it's the wall clock cap anyway, and connect is usually the bottleneck when offline.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. `--max-time 3` means the entire request must complete in 3s. `--connect-timeout 3` means the connect phase must complete in 3s. If we set connect-timeout to 3s and max-time to 3s, there's zero room for the actual HTTP request after connecting. But for `/v1/models`, the response is tiny and instant, so 3s is fine.

But the user is asking specifically about connect-timeout. Since we're passing it through as a parameter to json_client, and the config is already passing `3` for timeout_s (max-time), let me add connect-timeout as a separate parameter or just hardcode a low connect-timeout in the config call.

Actually, the simplest approach: since `--connect-timeout` should be low and we're already calling `get_models` with a timeout, I should pass a connect timeout too. I could add a second parameter `connect_timeout_s` or just add `--connect-timeout "3"` alongside `--max-time "3"` in the config call.

Let me keep it simple and just add `--connect-timeout` with a sensible default in json_client, or pass it through.
messages.content:
3 seconds for `--connect-timeout`. That's enough for a TCP handshake on a healthy LAN, but fails fast if the host is down. And since `--max-time` is already 3s, connect timeout being 3s means the entire request (connect + HTTP) has 3s budget — which is fine for `/v1/models` since it's a tiny response.

Let me wire it up:

{"content":[{"type":"text","name":"EXIT_CODE","text":"0"},{"type":"text","name":"STDOUT","text":"local http = require("socket.http") -- luarocks install --local --lua-version=5.1 luasocket
-- BTW arch: sudo pacman --noconfirm -S lua51   -- to install lua 5.1
local ltn12 = require("ltn12") -- also from luasocket
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

local JsonClient = {
    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, timeout_s)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(timeout_s))
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
"}]}

messages.content:
Commit done. The change:

- `connect_timeout_s=3` — TCP handshake must succeed in 3s (fastest way to detect offline server)
- `max_timeout_s=3` — entire request (connect + HTTP) must complete in 3s (wall-clock cap)

If the server is down, the connect timeout fires first. If it's up but slow, max-time catches it. For agent/MCP activity, both are `nil` (no timeout).

{"content":[{"type":"text","name":"EXIT_CODE","text":"0"},{"type":"text","name":"STDOUT","text":"local http = require("socket.http") -- luarocks install --local --lua-version=5.1 luasocket
-- BTW arch: sudo pacman --noconfirm -S lua51   -- to install lua 5.1
local ltn12 = require("ltn12") -- also from luasocket
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

local JsonClient = {
    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param connect_timeout_s? integer @curl --connect-timeout in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@param max_timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, connect_timeout_s, max_timeout_s)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if connect_timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--connect-timeout")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(connect_timeout_s))
    end

    if max_timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(max_timeout_s))
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
"}]}

{"command_line":"cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/json_client.lua << 'EOF'
local http = require("socket.http") -- luarocks install --local --lua-version=5.1 luasocket
-- BTW arch: sudo pacman --noconfirm -S lua51   -- to install lua 5.1
local ltn12 = require("ltn12") -- also from luasocket
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

--- HTTP client options for curl timeouts.
---@class HttpTimeoutOptions
---@field connect_timeout? number @curl --connect-timeout in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@field max_time? number @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)

local JsonClient = {
    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param opts? HttpTimeoutOptions @optional timeout configuration (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, opts)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if opts then
        if opts.connect_timeout then
            table.insert(curl_args, "--connect-timeout")
            table.insert(curl_args, tostring(opts.connect_timeout))
        end
        if opts.max_time then
            table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
            table.insert(curl_args, tostring(opts.max_time))
        end
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
EOF"}

You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="qwen3.6-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen3.6-35b-a3b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>user

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮

    -- ...

```

```lua

    -- ...
end
```<|im_end|>

function M.ask_openai()
    local cmdline = vim.fn.getcmdline()
    print("asking...") -- overwrites showing luaeval("...") in cmdline

    local stdin_text = ' env: nvim (neovim) command mode (return a valid command w/o the leading : ) \n question: ' ..
        cmdline

    local suggest = require("ask-openai.cmdline.suggest")
    local result = suggest.get_vim_command_suggestion(stdin_text)
    return trim_null_characters(result)
end

---@param user_options AskOpenAIOptions
function M.setup(user_options)
    -- FYI this is called by the plugin consumer... passing their options
    config.setup(user_options)

    -- PRN feels like this belongs in cmdline_ask.setup()
    local lhs = config.get_options().keymaps.cmdline_ask
    if lhs then
        -- [e]valuate vimscript expression luaeval("...") which runs nested lua code
        -- DO NOT SET silent=true, messes up putting result into cmdline, also I wanna see print messages, IIUC that would be affected
        -- FYI `<C-\>e` is critical in the following, don't remove the `e` and `\\` is to escape the `\` in lua
        vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('c', lhs, '<C-\\>eluaeval("require(\'ask-openai\').ask_openai()")<CR>', { noremap = true, })
    else
        config.print_verbose("cmdline_ask keymap is disabled, skipping")
    end

    -- PRN feels like this belongs in predictions.setup()
    if config.local_share.are_predictions_enabled() then
        M.start_predictions()
    end

    require("ask-openai.frontends.context").setup()
    require("ask-openai.rewrites.frontend").setup()
    require("ask-openai.agents.frontend").setup()
    require("ask-openai.tools.mcp").setup()
    require("ask-openai.rag").setup()

    local function root_dir(bufnr, on_dir)
        -- NOTES:
        -- - FYI vim.lsp.config's root_dir func is NOT compatible with nvim-lspconfig's root_dir func
        -- - DO not use `root_marker` b/c it will start LSP with root_uri=None if it doesn't find the root marker! (YIKES)
        -- - use this `root_dir` function and just don't call `on_dir` if you don't want the LS for a given file

        --   - thus, F12 into a library => opens a separate LS instance for the library (also makes sense)
        --     - if library has its own RAG indexes => well, you might want to search those!

        -- * get root based on first .rag dir in/above file's directory
        local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
        local root = vim.fs.root(filepath, {
            ".rag", -- only if a .rag dir, otherwise we don't start this LS

            -- ".git", -- common, but in my case I need .rag (in fact, LS should never be setup if there's no .rag dir, so this would never be called)
        })

            -- ONLY start LS if you find a root
            -- FYI! if you see RAG results seemingly coming from a different repo, use this log to check located root:
            -- log:info(string.format("found ask-LS root=%s, bufnr=%d %s", tostring(root), bufnr, filepath))
            on_dir(root)

---@param user_options AskOpenAIOptions
function M.setup(user_options)
    -- FYI this is called by the plugin consumer... passing their options
    config.setup(user_options)

    -- PRN feels like this belongs in cmdline_ask.setup()
    local lhs = config.get_options().keymaps.cmdline_ask
    if lhs then
        -- [e]valuate vimscript expression luaeval("...") which runs nested lua code
        -- DO NOT SET silent=true, messes up putting result into cmdline, also I wanna see print messages, IIUC that would be affected
        -- FYI `<C-\>e` is critical in the following, don't remove the `e` and `\\` is to escape the `\` in lua
        vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('c', lhs, '<C-\\>eluaeval("require(\'ask-openai\').ask_openai()")<CR>', { noremap = true, })
    else
        config.print_verbose("cmdline_ask keymap is disabled, skipping")
    end

    -- PRN feels like this belongs in predictions.setup()
    if config.local_share.are_predictions_enabled() then<|im_end|>

uv.uv_process_t\n---@field pid? integer\n---@field start_time integer -- unix timestamp when request was sent (for timing)\n---@field marks_ns_id integer\n---@field type string\nlocal CurlRequest = {}\nlocal request_counter = 1\n\n---@class CurlRequestParams\n---@field body table<string, any>\n---@field base_url string\n---@field endpoint CompletionsEndpoints\n---@field type string\n\n---@param params CurlRequestParams\n---@return CurlRequest\nfunction CurlRequest:new(params)\n    self = setmetatable({}, { __index = self })\n    self.body = params.body\n    self.endpoint = params.endpoint\n    self.type = params.type or \"\"\n\n    local base_url = params.base_url\n    if base_url == nil or base_url == \"\" then\n        error(string.format(\"base_url must be set, currently is: %q\", base_url))\n    end\n    self.base_url = base_url\n\n    self.handle = nil\n    self.pid = nil\n    self.start_time = os.time()\n    self.marks_ns_id = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace(\"ask.marks.\" .. request_counter)\n    request_counter = request_counter + 1\n    return self\nend\n\n---@return string\nfunction CurlRequest:get_url()\n    return self.base_url .. self.endpoint\nend\n\nfunction CurlRequest.terminate(request)\n    if request == nil or request.handle == nil then\n        -- FYI prefer CurlRequest.terminate(request) b/c no error if request is nil\n        --   NOT request:terminate() -- get an error if request is nil\n        return\n    end\n\n    -- FYI handle/pid (this entire request object) is PER literal request\n    --  so no need to clear handle/pid\n    local handle = request.handle\n    if not handle:is_closing() then\n        -- log:trace(\"Terminating process, pid: \", request.pid)\n        -- sigterm is important to tell curl to stop the request, so the server doesn't keep generating!\n        handle:kill(\"sigterm\")\n        handle:close()\n    end\nend\n\nreturn CurlRequest\n"}]}

CurlRequestForTrace\n\n---@param request CurlRequest|CurlRequestForTrace\n---@param frontend StreamingFrontend\nfunction Curl.spawn(request, frontend)\n    request.body.stream = true\n\n    completion_logger.write_new_messages_jsonl(request, frontend)\n\n    local json_body = vim.json.encode(request.body)\n    local options = {\n        command = \"curl\",\n        args = {\n            \"--fail-with-body\",\n            \"-sSL\",\n            \"--no-buffer\", -- w/o this curl batches (test w/ `curl *` vs `curl * | cat` and you will see difference)\n            \"-X\", \"POST\",\n            request:get_url(),\n            \"-H\", \"Content-Type: application/json\",\n            \"-d\", json_body\n        },\n    }\n\n    ---@param data_value string\n    function on_data_sse(data_value)\n        -- FYI right now this function exists to catch unhandled errors and terminate\n        local success, error_message = safely.call(Curl.on_one_data_value, data_value, frontend, request)\n        if success then\n            return\n        end\n\n        -- request stops ASAP, but not immediately\n        CurlRequest.terminate(request)\n        frontend.explain_error(\"Abort... unhandled exception in curl: \" .. tostring(error_message))\n    end\n\n    local stdout = vim.uv.new_pipe(false)\n    local stderr = vim.uv.new_pipe(false)\n\n    local parser = SSEDataOnlyParser.new(on_data_sse)\n\n    ---@param code integer\n    ---@param signal integer\n    local function on_exit(code, signal)\n        log:trace_on_exit_always(code, signal)\n        -- log:trace_on_exit_errors(code, signal) -- less verbose\n\n        -- close before check dregs (b/c might still be data unflushed in STDOUT/ERR)\n        stdout:close()\n        stderr:close()\n\n        request.handle = nil\n        request.pid = nil\n\n        -- flush dregs before on_curl_exited_successfully\n        -- - which may depend on, for example, a tool_call in dregs\n        local error_text = parser:flush_dregs()\n        if error_text then\n            frontend.explain_error(error_text)\n        end\n\n        if code == 0 then\n            -- FYI this has to come after dregs which may have data used by exit handler!\n            --  i.e. triggering tool_calls\n            frontend.on_curl_exited_successfully()\n        end\n\n        -- FYI review proper uv.spawn cleanup LATER:\n        -- - review:   vim.loop.walk(function(handle) print(handle) end)\n        --   - I am seeing alot after I just startup nvim... I wonder if some are from my MCP tool comms?\n        --   - and what about my timer/schduling for debounced keyboard events to trigger predictions?\n        -- - REVIEW OTHER uses of uv.spawn (and timers)... for missing cleanup logic!)\n    end\n\n    request.handle, request.pid = vim.uv.spawn(options.command,\n        ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: missing-fields\n        {\n            args = options.args,\n            stdio = { nil, stdout, stderr },\n        },\n        on_exit)\n\n    ---@param read_error any\n    ---@param data? string\n    local function on_stdout(read_error, data)\n        -- log:log_if_stdio_read_error(\"on_stdout\", read_error, data)\n        log:trace_stdio_read_always(\"on_stdout\", read_error, data)\n\n        local no_data = data == nil or data == \"\"\n        if read_error or no_data then\n            return\n        end\n        assert(data ~= nil)\n\n        parser:write(data)\n    end\n    stdout:read_start(on_stdout)\n\n    ---@param read_error? string\n    ---@param data? string\n    local function on_stderr(read_error, data)\n        -- log:log_if_stdio_read_error(\"on_stderr\", read_error, data)\n        log:trace_stdio_read_always(\"on_stderr\", read_error, data)\n\n        local no_data = data == nil or data == \"\"\n        if read_error or no_data then\n            return\n        end\n        assert(data ~= nil)\n\n        -- keep in mind... curl errors will show as text in STDERR\n        frontend.explain_error(data)\n    end\n    stderr:read_start(on_stderr)\nend\n\n---@param data_value string\n---@param frontend StreamingFrontend\nfunction Curl.on_one_data_value(data_value, frontend, request)\n    -- log:trace(\"data_value\", data_value)\n\n    if data_value == \"[DONE]\" then\n        -- log:trace(\"DETECTED DONE\")\n        return\n    end\n\n    -- * PARSE DATA VALUE (JSON) => sse_parsed object\n    local success, sse_parsed = safely.decode_json(data_value)\n    if success and sse_parsed then\n        frontend.on_parsed_data_sse(sse_parsed)\n        -- FYI not every SSE has to have generated tokens (choices), no need to warn if no parsed value\n        completion_logger.log_sse_to_request(sse_parsed, request, frontend)\n\n        if sse_parsed.error then\n            -- only confirmed this on llama_server\n            -- {\"error\":{\"code\":500,\"message\":\"tools param requires --jinja flag\",\"type\":\"server_error\"}}\n            -- DO NOT LOG HERE TOO\n            frontend.explain_error(\"found error on what looks like an SSE:\" .. vim.inspect(sse_parsed))\n        end\n\n        if sse_parsed.timings then\n            frontend.on_sse_llama_server_timings(sse_parsed)\n        end\n    else\n        -- PRN in the spirit of triggering events for scenarios, I could add:\n        --  frontend:on_data_value_parse_failure(data_value)\n        --  but central logging has been fine so far\n        log:warn(\"SSE json parse failed for data_value: \", data_value)\n    end\nend\n\nreturn Curl\n"}]}

(YIKES)\n        -- - use this `root_dir` function and just don't call `on_dir` if you don't want the LS for a given file\n        -- - finds workspace root for *EACH BUFFER*\n        --   - each root discovered == new LS instance (makes sense)\n        --   - thus, F12 into a library => opens a separate LS instance for the library (also makes sense)\n        --     - if library has its own RAG indexes => well, you might want to search those!\n\n        -- * get root based on first .rag dir in/above file's directory\n        local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)\n        local root = vim.fs.root(filepath, {\n            \".rag\", -- only if a .rag dir, otherwise we don't start this LS\n            -- FYI might have issues w/ nested .rag dirs but I don't really use that... handle if it arises\n            -- \".git\", -- common, but in my case I need .rag (in fact, LS should never be setup if there's no .rag dir, so this would never be called)\n        })\n\n        if root then\n            -- ONLY start LS if you find a root\n            -- FYI! if you see RAG results seemingly coming from a different repo, use this log to check located root:\n            -- log:info(string.format(\"found ask-LS root=%s, bufnr=%d %s\", tostring(root), bufnr, filepath))\n            on_dir(root)\n            return\n        end\n        log:warn(string.format(\"no ask-LS root found for bufnr=%d %s\", bufnr, filepath))\n    end\n\n    vim.lsp.config(\"ask_language_server\", {\n\n        -- * language server\n        cmd = {\n            os.getenv(\"HOME\") .. \"/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/.venv/bin/python\",\n            \"-m\",\n            \"lsp.server\",\n        },\n        cmd_cwd = os.getenv(\"HOME\") .. \"/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rag\",\n\n        -- old values from lspconfig setup => these might need adjusted if filetypes differs in vim.lsp.config\n        -- filetypes = rag_client.get_filetypes_for_workspace(),\n        -- filetypes = { '*' }, -- not set == all filetypes\n\n        root_dir = root_dir\n    })\n    -- log:info(vim.inspect(vim.lsp.config))\n    vim.lsp.enable(\"ask_language_server\")\n    -- FYI :checkhealth vim.lsp  \" shows enabled vs active (buffer #s too)\n\n    -- ---@param result { message: string, type: number }  -- Language Server MessageType\n    -- local function map_lsp_level_to_vim_level(result)\n"}]}

It works with Lua 5.1+ and avoids the C compiler\n-- hassle required by `cmsgpack`. Ensure it is installed via:\n--   luarocks install --lua-version=5.1 lua-MessagePack\n\nlocal M = {}\n\n--- Default connection options – can be overridden via `setup`.\n---@type table\nlocal opts = {\n    host = \"ask.lan\",\n    port = 8015,\n    timeout = 5, -- seconds for connect/read/write\n}\n\n--- Configure the client.\n--- Allows the caller to set host, port and timeout.\n---@param user_opts table\nfunction M.setup(user_opts)\n    if type(user_opts) ~= \"table\" then return end\n    for k, v in pairs(user_opts) do\n        opts[k] = v\n    end\nend\n\n--- Internal helper: establish a TCP connection.\n---@return socket.tcp|nil, string|nil\nlocal function connect()\n    local tcp = assert(socket.tcp())\n    tcp:settimeout(opts.timeout)\n    local ok, err = tcp:connect(opts.host, opts.port)\n    if not ok then\n        return nil, (\"embeddings connect error: \" .. (err or \"unknown\"))\n    end\n    return tcp, nil\nend\n\n--- Internal helper: send a MessagePack‑encoded request.\n---@param tcp socket.tcp\n---@param payload table\n---@return boolean, string|nil\nlocal function send_message(tcp, payload)\n    local data = mp.pack(payload)\n    -- prefix with 4‑byte big‑endian length as expected by the server\n    local len = #data\n    local prefix = string.char(\n        bit.rshift(bit.band(len, 0xFF000000), 24),\n        bit.rshift(bit.band(len, 0x00FF0000), 16),\n        bit.rshift(bit.band(len, 0x0000FF00), 8),\n        bit.band(len, 0x000000FF)\n    )\n    local ok, err = tcp:send(prefix .. data)\n    if not ok then\n        return false, (\"embeddings send error: \" .. (err or \"unknown\"))\n    end\n    return true, nil\nend\n\n--- Internal helper: receive a length‑prefixed MessagePack response.\n---@param tcp socket.tcp\n---@return table|nil, string|nil\nlocal function receive_message(tcp)\n    local header, err = tcp:receive(4)\n    if not header then\n        return nil, (\"embeddings recv header error: \" .. (err or \"unknown\"))\n    end\n    local b1, b2, b3, b4 = header:byte(1, 4)\n    local len = b1 * 0x1000000 + b2 * 0x10000 + b3 * 0x100 + b4\n    local payload, err2 = tcp:receive(len)\n    if not payload then\n        return nil, (\"embeddings recv payload error: \" .. (err2 or \"unknown\"))\n    end\n    local ok, decoded = pcall(mp.unpack, payload)\n    if not ok then\n        return nil, (\"embeddings unpack error: \" .. (decoded or \"unknown\"))\n    end\n    return decoded, nil\nend\n\n--- Request an embedding for a single string.\n---@param text string\n---@return number[]|nil, string|nil\nfunction M.embed_text(text)\n    if type(text) ~= \"string\" then\n        return nil, \"embed_text expects a string\"\n    end\n    local tcp, err = connect()\n    if not tcp then return nil, err end\n    local ok, send_err = send_message(tcp, { text = text })\n    if not ok then return nil, send_err end\n    local resp, recv_err = receive_message(tcp)\n    tcp:close()\n    if not resp then return nil, recv_err end\n    if resp.error then return nil, resp.error end\n    return resp.embeddings, nil\nend\n\n--- Request embeddings for a batch of strings, splitting into sub‑batches when needed.\n---@param texts string[] @list of strings to embed\n---@param batch_size? integer @size of each sub‑batch (default 8)\n---@return table[]|nil, string|nil @list of embedding vectors or error message\nfunction M.embed_batch(texts, batch_size)\n    batch_size = batch_size or 8\n\n    if type(texts) ~= \"table\" then\n        return nil, \"embed_batch expects a table of strings\"\n    end\n\n    local total = #texts\n    local all_vecs = {}\n\n    for i = 1, total, batch_size do\n        local batch_end = math.min(i + batch_size - 1, total)\n        local batch = {}\n        for j = i, batch_end do\n            table.insert(batch, texts[j])\n        end\n\n        local msg = { texts = batch, type = \"embed\" }\n\n        vim.print(string.format(\"batch %d-%d of %d\", i, batch_end, total))\n\n        -- Open a new connection for each sub‑batch\n        local tcp, err = connect()\n        if not tcp then\n            vim.print(\"  TCP.connect \", vim.inspect(tcp), vim.inspect(err))\n            return nil, err\n        end\n\n        local ok, send_err = send_message(tcp, msg)\n        if not ok then\n            vim.print(\"  send_message error: \", vim.inspect(ok), vim.inspect(send_err))\n            tcp:close()\n            return nil, send_err\n        end\n\n        local resp, recv_err = receive_message(tcp)\n        tcp:close()\n        if not resp then\n            vim.print(\"  receive_message error: \", vim.inspect(recv_err))\n            return nil, recv_err\n        end\n        if resp.error then\n            vim.print(\"  embed_batch error: \", vim.inspect(resp.error))\n            return nil, resp.error\n        end\n\n        table.insert(all_vecs, resp.embeddings)\n    end\n\n    -- Concatenate results from all sub‑batches\n    local concatenated = {}\n    for _, vecs in ipairs(all_vecs) do\n        for _, v in ipairs(vecs) do\n            table.insert(concatenated, v)\n        end\n    end\n\n    return concatenated, nil\nend\n\nfunction M.qwen3_format_query(query, instruct)\n    if instruct ~= nil then\n        -- FYI verbatim copy of formatter from embedder.py...\n        --   was the lack of \" \" after Query: in the original examples?\n        return \"Instruct: \" .. instruct .. \"\\nQuery:\" .. query\n    end\n    return \"Query: \" .. query\nend\n\nreturn M\n"}]}

Root cause found. Let me trace the exact call chain:

1. **`agents/frontend.lua` line 29** runs at module load:
   ```lua
   AgentsFrontend.base_url = config.get_endpoints().agents.base_url
   ```

2. **`config/init.lua` line 155** calls:
   ```lua
   local gptoss_model = M.get_llama_server_model(gptoss_url)
   local qwen3_model = M.get_llama_server_model(qwen3_url)
   ```

3. **`config/init.lua` line 25** calls:
   ```lua
   local response = LlamaServerClient.get_models(base_url)
   ```

4. **`backends/json_client.lua` line 43** calls:
   ```lua
   local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)
   ```

The `curl` command in `json_client.lua` has **NO `-m` / `--max-time` flag**, so when `ask.lan:8012` and `ask.lan:8013` are offline, curl hangs indefinitely, blocking Neovim startup.

Here's the fix:

<tool_call>
<function=run_process>
<parameter=command_line>
head -50 ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/json_client.lua
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

<|im_start|>assistant
Here's the root cause and fix:

## Root Cause

The startup hang was caused by `curl` being called **without a `--max-time` flag** in `json_client.lua`, which is called during Neovim startup via this chain:

1. `agents/frontend.lua:29` (module load) → calls `config.get_endpoints().agents.base_url`
2. `config/init.lua:155` → calls `M.get_llama_server_model("http://ask.lan:8012")` and `get_llama_server_model("http://ask.lan:8013")`
3. `config/init.lua:25` → calls `LlamaServerClient.get_models(base_url)`
4. `backends/json_client.lua:43` → runs `vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)` **with no timeout**

When `ask.lan:8012` or `ask.lan:8013` are unreachable, curl hangs indefinitely waiting for a connection, blocking all of Neovim startup.

## Fix

Added `--max-time` to all three places where `curl` is invoked without a timeout:

| File | Timeout | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `backends/json_client.lua` | 3s | Startup model discovery (fast fail) |
| `backends/curl.lua` | 120s | Streaming completions |
| `tools/mcp/init.lua` | 10s | MCP HTTP server communication |<|im_end|>

Curl doesn't have a native "idle timeout" — `--max-time` is wall-clock time. The only way to do idle detection would be external monitoring of the process, which isn't worth it.

For agent/MCP activity, no timeout is the right call. Let me revert those two:

cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/json_client.lua << 'EOF'
local http = require("socket.http") -- luarocks install --local --lua-version=5.1 luasocket
-- BTW arch: sudo pacman --noconfirm -S lua51   -- to install lua 5.1
local ltn12 = require("ltn12") -- also from luasocket
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

local JsonClient = {
    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, timeout_s)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(timeout_s))
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
EOF
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

local json_client = require("ask-openai.backends.json_client")

local LlamaServerClient = {
    ENDPOINTS = {
        URL_V1_MODELS = "/v1/models",
        URL_V1_CHAT_COMPLETIONS = "/v1/chat/completions",
        URL_APPLY_TEMPLATE = "/apply-template",
    }
}

---@param base_url string
---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.v1_chat_completions(base_url, body, timeout_s)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_V1_CHAT_COMPLETIONS
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.POST, body, timeout_s)
end

---@param base_url string
---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.apply_template(base_url, body, timeout_s)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_APPLY_TEMPLATE
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.POST, body, timeout_s)
end

---@param base_url string
---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.get_models(base_url, timeout_s)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_V1_MODELS
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.GET, nil, timeout_s)
end

return LlamaServerClient
EOF

1. **`json_client.lua`** - `timeout_s` is now an optional parameter (default `nil` = no timeout)
2. **`llama_server_client.lua`** - all methods accept and pass through `timeout_s`

I want that low b/c that would be the fastest way to abort if the server is not online which is most likely in my case to be a problem... if it is online then it will almost always respond quickly for model name... so connect timeout should be low for getting name... what value would you suggest for connect timeout?<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>assistant
3 seconds for `--connect-timeout`. That's enough for a TCP handshake on a healthy LAN, but fails fast if the host is down. And since `--max-time` is already 3s, connect timeout being 3s means the entire request (connect + HTTP) has 3s budget — which is fine for `/v1/models` since it's a tiny response.

table\n---@param timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)\n---@return JsonClientResponse?\nfunction JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, timeout_s)\n    local request_json = nil\n    if request_body then\n        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)\n    end\n\n    local curl_args = {\n        \"curl\",\n        \"-sS\",                      -- silent but show errors\n        \"-X\", method,\n        \"-H\", \"Content-Type: application/json\",\n    }\n\n    if timeout_s then\n        table.insert(curl_args, \"--max-time\")\n        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(timeout_s))\n    end\n\n    if request_json then\n        table.insert(curl_args, \"-d\")\n        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)\n    end\n\n    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line\n    table.insert(curl_args, url)\n    table.insert(curl_args, \"-w\")\n    table.insert(curl_args, \"\\n%{http_code}\")\n\n    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines\n    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)\n\n    -- The last line is the HTTP status code\n    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0\n    -- All preceding lines form the response body\n    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line\n    local body_str = table.concat(output, \"\\n\")\n\n    ---@class JsonClientResponse\n    ---@field code integer\n    ---@field body any|nil\n    local response = {\n        code = http_code,\n        body = (function()\n            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)\n            if ok then\n                return body\n            end\n            return nil\n        end)(),\n    }\n\n    -- vim.print(response)\n    return response\nend\n\nreturn JsonClient\n"}]}

    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param connect_timeout_s? integer @curl --connect-timeout in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@param max_timeout_s? integer @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, connect_timeout_s, max_timeout_s)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if connect_timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--connect-timeout")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(connect_timeout_s))
    end

    if max_timeout_s then
        table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
        table.insert(curl_args, tostring(max_timeout_s))
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
EOF
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

Commit done. The change:

- `connect_timeout_s=3` — TCP handshake must succeed in 3s (fastest way to detect offline server)
- `max_timeout_s=3` — entire request (connect + HTTP) must complete in 3s (wall-clock cap)

If the server is down, the connect timeout fires first. If it's up but slow, max-time catches it. For agent/MCP activity, both are `nil` (no timeout).<|im_end|>

cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/json_client.lua << 'EOF'
local http = require("socket.http") -- luarocks install --local --lua-version=5.1 luasocket
-- BTW arch: sudo pacman --noconfirm -S lua51   -- to install lua 5.1
local ltn12 = require("ltn12") -- also from luasocket
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

--- HTTP client options for curl timeouts.
---@class HttpTimeoutOptions
---@field connect_timeout? number @curl --connect-timeout in seconds (nil = no timeout)
---@field max_time? number @curl --max-time in seconds (nil = no timeout)

local JsonClient = {
    ---@enum Methods
    Methods = {
        GET = "GET",
        POST = "POST",
    }
}

--- Send an HTTP request via curl and return the JSON response body.
---@param url string
---@param method Methods
---@param request_body? table
---@param opts? HttpTimeoutOptions @optional timeout configuration (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function JsonClient.get_response_body(url, method, request_body, opts)
    local request_json = nil
    if request_body then
        request_json = vim.json.encode(request_body)
    end

    local curl_args = {
        "curl",
        "-sS",                      -- silent but show errors
        "-X", method,
        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
    }

    if opts then
        if opts.connect_timeout then
            table.insert(curl_args, "--connect-timeout")
            table.insert(curl_args, tostring(opts.connect_timeout))
        end
        if opts.max_time then
            table.insert(curl_args, "--max-time")
            table.insert(curl_args, tostring(opts.max_time))
        end
    end

    if request_json then
        table.insert(curl_args, "-d")
        table.insert(curl_args, request_json)
    end

    -- Append URL and ask curl to output HTTP status code on a new line
    table.insert(curl_args, url)
    table.insert(curl_args, "-w")
    table.insert(curl_args, "\n%{http_code}")

    -- Execute curl and capture output as a list of lines
    local output = vim.fn.systemlist(curl_args)

    -- The last line is the HTTP status code
    local http_code = tonumber(output[#output]) or 0
    -- All preceding lines form the response body
    table.remove(output)                     -- drop the status line
    local body_str = table.concat(output, "\n")

    ---@class JsonClientResponse
    ---@field code integer
    ---@field body any|nil
    local response = {
        code = http_code,
        body = (function()
            local ok, body = safely.decode_json(body_str)
            if ok then
                return body
            end
            return nil
        end)(),
    }

    -- vim.print(response)
    return response
end

return JsonClient
EOF
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

local json_client = require("ask-openai.backends.json_client")

local LlamaServerClient = {
    ENDPOINTS = {
        URL_V1_MODELS = "/v1/models",
        URL_V1_CHAT_COMPLETIONS = "/v1/chat/completions",
        URL_APPLY_TEMPLATE = "/apply-template",
    }
}

---@param base_url string
---@param body table
---@param opts? HttpTimeoutOptions @optional timeout configuration (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.v1_chat_completions(base_url, body, opts)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_V1_CHAT_COMPLETIONS
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.POST, body, opts)
end

---@param base_url string
---@param body table
---@param opts? HttpTimeoutOptions @optional timeout configuration (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.apply_template(base_url, body, opts)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_APPLY_TEMPLATE
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.POST, body, opts)
end

---@param base_url string
---@param opts? HttpTimeoutOptions @optional timeout configuration (nil = no timeout)
---@return JsonClientResponse?
function LlamaServerClient.get_models(base_url, opts)
    local url = base_url .. LlamaServerClient.ENDPOINTS.URL_V1_MODELS
    return json_client.get_response_body(url, json_client.Methods.GET, nil, opts)
end

return LlamaServerClient
EOF
